title: Carts, Checkout, Orders Introduction
description: A cart contains a list of the products that a shopper adds to the cart while browsing your catalog. In the context of a cart, a selected product is called a cart item.

email.description: The email address attached to a cart.

custom_discounts_enabled.description: This parameter enables custom discounts for a cart. When set to true, Elastic Path promotions will not be applied to the new carts. Default is set from cart discount settings for the store. See [Cart Settings](/docs/api/carts/put-v-2-settings-cart).

use_rule_promotions.description: When set to true, this parameter allows the cart to use rule promotions.

defer_inventory_check.description: This parameter enables deferring inventory checks on the cart. This allows items to be added to or updating in the cart without checking stock levels. Inventory checks are still performed as normal on checkout

separate_items_by_location.description: When set to true, cart items with the same SKU but different locations are kept as separate line items instead of being merged. When false (default), items with the same SKU are merged and the location is updated to the most recent value.

CustomAttributes.title: CustomAttributes

type.description: The type of the custom attribute value.

value.description: The value of the custom attribute.

currency.description: The currency set for this amount.

formatted.description: The formatted total based on the amount and currency.

updated_at.description: The date this was last updated.

type.description: The type of related resource.

id.description: The ID of the related resource.
The unique identifier for the cart. Use SDK or create it yourself.

payment_intent_id.description: Stripe-assigned unique identifier for the linked Payment Intent

snapshot_date.description: The snapshot date for the cart.

self.description: A link to that specific resource.

auto_add_free_gift.description: Whether to automatically add free gift

cart_item_id.description: Cart item ID for the target

last.description: If there is only one page, it is `null`.

next.description: If there is only one page, it is `null`.

prev.description: if the user is on the first page, it is `null`.

limit.description: The maximum number of records per page for this response. You can set this value up to 100.

offset.description: The current offset by number of records, not pages. Offset is zero-based.

title.description: A brief summary of the error.

status.description: The HTTP response code of the error.

detail.description: Optional additional detail about the error.

source.description: The field or location that caused the validation error. For JSON schema validation errors, this contains the JSON path to the invalid field (e.g., 'data.name', 'request', 'data.items[0].quantity').

meta.description: Additional metadata associated with the error. May include arbitrary keys.

id.description: The resource id associated with the error

items.description: The resource ids associated with the error
The cart_item ids associated with the error

shipping_group_id.description: The shipping group id associated with the error

items.description: The shipping group ids associated with the error

cart_id.description: The cart id associated with the error

code.description: The code associated with the error.

order_id.description: The order id associated with the error.

sku.description: The SKU associated with the error.

email.description: The email address associated with the error.

component_product_id.description: The component product ID associated with the error.

error-meta-key.description: Custom error metadata key used for additional error context (e.g., in payment rejections).

value.description: The value associated with the error.

name.description: The cart name provided by the shopper. A cart name must contain 1 to 255 characters. You cannot use whitespace characters, but special characters are permitted. For more information, see the [Safe Characters](/guides/Getting-Started/safe-characters) section.

snapshot_date.description: This optional parameter sets a reference date for the cart. If this parameter is set, it allows the cart to act as one that might occur on that specified date. For example, such future carts might acquire future-enabled discounts, allowing users to test and validate future interactions with carts. The snapshot_date must be in the format 2026-02-21T15:07:25Z. By default, this parameter is left empty.

custom_attributes.description:
Specifies custom attributes for cart objects. Each attribute includes a top-level key, as well as corresponding type and value entries. Attribute values must correspond to the assigned types.

Attribute types include:
  - string
  - boolean
  - integer
  - float

Multiple custom attributes may be submitted together.  A cart can have a maximum of 20 custom attributes.

Example:
```
"custom_attributes": {
  "is_member": {
    "type": "boolean",
    "value": true
  },
  "membership_level": {
    "type": "string",
    "value": "premium"
  }
}
```

Updating an existing cart with new custom attributes clears previously-saved attributes.  In order to maintain existing custom attributes on a cart, please include them in the update request along with any new attributes.

payment_intent_id.description: To remove the Stripe payment intent from a cart, pass the empty value in the `payment_intent_id` field.  You must use an empty value for this field. You cannot use this endpoint to directly update the cart to use an existing Payment Intent.

UpdateAllOrNothingOptionsObject.title: Update All Or Nothing Options Object

update_all_or_nothing.description: When set to`true`, if an error occurs for any item, no items are updated in the cart. When set to `false`, valid items are updated in the cart and the items with errors are reported in the response. Default is `true`.

quantity.description: The number of items to add.

id.description: Specifies the ID of the product you want to add. (use this OR sku)

sku.description: Specifies the item SKU that you want to add. (use this OR id)

name.description: Optional name for the product item. If not provided, the product name from catalog will be used.

custom_inputs.description: The custom text to be added to a product.

bundle_configuration.description: Object used to describe the bundle options selected.

component_products.description: Array of component products for the selected options.

location.description: The slug of a stock location.

AddAllOrNothingOptionsObject.title: Add All Or Nothing Options Object

add_all_or_nothing.description: When `true`, if an error occurs for any item, no items are added to the cart. When `false`, valid items are added to the cart and the items with errors are reported in the response. Default is `false`.

ElasticPathPaymentsPoweredByStripePaymen.title: Elastic Path Payments Powered By Stripe

gateway.description: Specifies the gateway. You must use `elastic_path_payments_stripe`.

receipt_email.description: Provides the email address to which you want to send the Stripe receipts for the transactions within the store. This feature is available only in the live mode.

automatic_payment_methods.description: Parent object determining whether to use Stripe's `automatic_payment_methods` setting.

enabled.description: When set to true, it displays all enabled payment methods from the Stripe dashboard. When set to false, the Stripe default, which is card, is used.

payment_method_types.description: Specifies the Stripe payment method types configured for the store. See [Stripe Documentation](https://docs.stripe.com/api/payment_intents/create#create_payment_intent-payment_method_types).

payment.description: Specifies the Stripe token or source.

BillingAddress.title: BillingAddress
BillingAddress.description: Billing address information. You must specify at least one of region (state/province) or county.

order_number.description: A user-managed, optional field used as an alternative to the existing `order_id`. If provided, the order-number will be sent to Authorize.net instead of the `order_id`, and will appear as the invoice number in Authorize.net transactions.

external_ref.description: An optional external ID reference for an order. It can contain alphanumeric characters, special characters, and spaces, and is not required to be unique. The maximum allowed length is 64 characters. It can be used to include an external reference from a separate company system.

FailureDetails.title: FailureDetails
FailureDetails.description: Non-PII payment failure information from the payment gateway. Only present on failed transactions where the gateway provided structured failure details.

code.description: Machine-readable failure or error code from the payment gateway.

reason.description: Human-readable failure reason or error message from the payment gateway.

id.description: The ID of the transaction.

name.description: A custom name associated with the payment method.

custom_reference.description: A reference associated with the payment method. This might include loyalty points or gift card identifiers. We recommend you not to include personal information in this field.

gateway.description: The name of the payment gateway used.

transaction_type.description: The type of transaction, such as `purchase`, `capture`, `authorize` or `refund`.

type.description: The type of object being returned.

status.description: The status provided by the gateway for this transaction, such as `complete` or `failed`.

detailed_status.description: Detailed status from the payment gateway.

failure_details.description: Non-PII payment failure details from the gateway. Only present on failed transactions where the gateway provided structured failure information.

capture_mechanism.description: The mechanism used for capturing the transaction.

refund_mechanism.description: The mechanism used for refunding the transaction.

redirect_url.description: URL to redirect the client after payment.

next_actions.description: Array of next actions for the transaction.

payment.description: The Braintree Customer ID that you want to bill.

gateway.description: Specifies the gateway. You must use `card_connect`.

payment.description: Enter account_id, profile_id from CardPointe API. For example, 1|16178397535388255208.

gateway.description: Specifies the gateway. You must use `cyber_source`.
Specifies the type of payment gateway. You must use `manual`.

custom_reference.description: A reference associated with the payment method. This might include loyalty points or gift card identifiers. We recommend not to include personal information in this field.

name.description: A custom name associated with the payment method.

gateway.description: Specifies the type of payment gateway. You must use `paypal_express_checkout`.

soft_descriptor.description: The descriptor appended to PayPal generated descriptor that is visible on the card statement of the payer.

brand_name.description: The label that overrides the business name in the PayPal account on the PayPal site.

locale.description: The locale pages that appear based on language and country code. PayPal supports a five-character code. For example, ja-JP.

landing_page.description: The type of landing page to show on the PayPal site for customer checkout. Use values LOGIN, BILLING, or NO_PREFERENCE.

shipping_preference.description: The shipping preference. Use SET_PROVIDED_ADDRESS value. This parameter does allow the user to change their address on PayPal site.

user_action.description: If you set `useraction=commit` in the query string, the flow redirects the buyer to the PayPal payment page and displays a Pay Now button. When the shopper clicks **Pay Now**, call `DoExpressCheckoutPayment` to complete the payment without additional interaction from the shopper. Choose this flow when you know the final payment amount when you initiate the checkout flow.

return_url.description: The callback URL for PayPal to redirect the user in the case of approved payment.

cancel_url.description: The callback URL for PayPal to redirect user in the case a cancelled payment.

gateway.description: Specifies the type of payment gateway. You must use `stripe`.

receipt_email.description: The option to provide an email for Stripe receipts. Specify live mode to access this feature.

gateway.description: Specifies the type of payment gateway. You must use `stripe_payment_intents`.

receipt_email.description: Provides the email address to which you want to send the Stripe receipts for the transactions within the store. This feature is available only in the live mode.

payment.description: Specifies the Stripe token or source.

amount.description: The amount value to be refunded. If this field is not provided, it will be considered as manual refund (Mark as Refunded) and the refund process must be manually handled via payment provider. If the amount value is same as payment value, then it will be treated as a full refund and sent to the payment provider to process refund automatically.

note.description: Provides comments about the refund. It is used by PayPal Express.

reason.description: Specifies the reason for canceling the transaction. The reason may include `duplicate`, `fraudulent`, `requested_by_customer`, or `abandoned`.

external_ref.description: An optional external ID reference for a shipping group. It can contain alphanumeric characters, special characters, and spaces, and is not required to be unique. The maximum allowed length is 64 characters.

type.description: Describes the type of request payload you're sending. Set this value to `settings`.

id.description: The unique identifier for the cart settings.

cart_expiry_days.description: Indicates the number of days before a cart expires.

custom_discounts_enabled.description: When `true`, custom discounts are enabled. Default is false. This setting only affects the new empty carts while the existing active carts will not be affected.

use_rule_promotions.description: When set to `true`, this parameter allows the cart to use rule promotions.

defer_inventory_check.description: When `true`, inventory checks are not performed when adding products to a cart but still occur on checkout as normal. Default is false. This setting only affects the new empty carts while the existing active carts will not be affected.

separate_items_by_location.description: When `true`, cart items with the same SKU but different locations are kept as separate line items instead of being merged. When `false` (default), items with the same SKU are merged and the location is updated to the most recent value.

show_all_carts.description:
When `true`, admins (users with admin scope) can retrieve all carts in the store via `GET /v2/carts`, regardless of customer or account associations. When `false`, admins can only see carts that are registered (associated with customers or accounts).

For new stores, this defaults to `true`. For existing stores created before this feature was introduced, this defaults to `false` to maintain backward compatibility.
